post.description:
Place an order into the matching engine queue, conditional on validation. A successful response indicates that the order was placed in the queue, but does **not** guarantee that it will be filled or placed on the order book.
To reliably ensure that an order has been executed, consume messages from the matching tape to reconstruct the order book and other stateful structures.
> **Note:** If an order placed wash trades against an order from the same user, both orders will wash fill each other and effectively be cancelled.

**Rate limit:** 4 requests per 100 milliseconds.

Submit multiple orders (maximum 256) at once to be processed in a batch. This operation does not make guarantees about the successful execution of all orders, it only places each in the matching engine queue.

**Rate limit:** 4 requests per 100 milliseconds.

delete.description:
Cancel multiple orders (maximum 256) by their IDs. The orders must belong to the trader associated with the API key. This operation is idempotent, but does not make guarantees about reaching the matching engine before the orders are filled.
To reliably ensure that orders have been cancelled, consume messages from the matching tape to reconstruct the order book and other stateful structures.
> **Note:** A successful response indicates that the cancellation requests were sent to the queue, but does **not** guarantee that the orders will be cancelled.

**Rate limit:** 1,000 requests per second.

items.description: The server-generated UUID of the order to be cancelled

200.description: Cancellation requests sent to the queue.

delete.description:
Cancel a specific order by its unique identifier. The order must belong to the trader associated with the API key. This operation is idempotent, but does not make guarantees about reaching the matching engine before the order is filled.
To reliably ensure that an order has been cancelled, consume messages from the matching tape to reconstruct the order book and other stateful structures.
> **Note:** A successful response indicates that the cancellation request was sent to the queue, but does **not** guarantee that the order will be cancelled.

**Rate limit:** 1,000 requests per second.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: The server-generated UUID of the order to be cancelled

get.description:
Fetch details of a specific order using its unique identifier.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: The server-generated UUID of the order to retrieve

delete.description:
Cancel all active orders for the user, satisfying the provided parameters. This operation is idempotent, but does not make guarantees about reaching the matching engine before the orders are filled.

**Rate limit:** 1 request per 30 seconds.

200.description: Cancellation requests sent to the queue.

get.description:
Get a list of all orders associated with the user, satisfying the provided parameters. Results are limited to a maximum of 100 items by default.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset of the orders to return

200.description: Successfully retrieved all orders for the user

get.description:
Access a complete list of all fills for the user, satisfying the provided parameters. This operation provides detailed records of each fill, including the quantity, price, and time of execution. Results are limited to a maximum of 100 items by default.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset of the fills to return

200.description: Successfully retrieved all fills for the user

get.description:
Get a comprehensive overview of all positions held by the user, satisfying the provided parameters. This operation includes details about each position's size, entry price, and current market value. Results are limited to a maximum of 100 items by default.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

parameters.name: outcomeIds
parameters.description: Array of outcome IDs to filter positions by. If omitted, you will get all positions across open markets.

Fetch information for a specific market using its unique identifier.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

Fetch a list of all markets currently open, satisfying the provided parameters.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

get.description:
Get the current order book for a specific market. For both outcomes of the market, orders are returned sorted by price-time priority. Orders on the book are partially obfuscated up to price, quantity, and direction.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

Get the current balance of the user's wallet under the given currency denomination.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

Get the user's open position cost, open order cost, and credit for the given currency denomination.

> **Note:** Data may be up to 5 minutes behind due to caching.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

200.description: Successfully retrieved open costs and credit.

get.description:
Get a detailed history of all transactions made by the user, satisfying the provided parameters. Results are limited to a maximum of 100 items by default.

**Rate limit:** 100 requests per second.

parameters.description: Offset of the transactions to return

info.title: NBX API
info.description:
## API Overview

The NBX API is designed with a tri-interface architecture:

- **REST API**
  - **URL:**          `https://api.novig.us/nbx/v2`
  - **Constraints:**
    - **Rate limits:**
      - Order placement: 4 requests per 100 milliseconds
      - Order cancellation: 1,000 requests per second
      - Kill switch: 1 request per 30 seconds
      - Data retrieval: 100 requests per second
    - **Timeout:**    5 seconds
  - **Features:**
    - Place, cancel, and query orders.
    - Retrieve market data and positions.
    - Manage account settings and balances.
  - **Best Practices:**
    - Use appropriate HTTP methods.
    - Include `Authorization` and `Content-Type` headers.
    - Implement exponential backoff for handling rate limits and robust error handling.

- **WebSocket API**
  - **URL:**          `wss://api.novig.us/tape`
  - **Constraints:**
    - **Rate limit:** 10 messages per minute
    - **Timeout:**    300 seconds without heartbeat
  - **Features:**
    - Real-time order book ticks.
    - Real-time market lifecycle updates.
    - Private fill and cancel notifications.
    - Heartbeat mechanism to ensure connection health.
  - **Best Practices:**
    - Implement reconnection logic with exponential backoff.
    - Process messages sequentially to maintain order book integrity.
    - Appropriately handle various event types.
    - Subscribe to specific market events or to the global tape.

- **GraphQL API**
  - **URL:**          `https://gql.novig.us/v1/graphql`
  - **Constraints:**
    - **Rate limit:** 650 requests per minute
    - **Timeout:**    2 seconds
  - **Features:**
    - Interactive Explorer available via [**Hoppscotch GraphQL Sandbox**](https://hoppscotch.io/graphql)
    - Flexible querying of market data and prices with tailored filters.
    - Access historical data and statistics.
    - Role-based access control with baseline lurker permissions.
  - **Best Practices:**
    - Use GraphQL for historical analysis and non-time-critical queries.
    - Opt for the WebSocket API for real-time data needs.

## Authentication

The NBX API uses [**OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials**](https://auth0.com/docs/get-started/authentication-and-authorization-flow/client-credentials-flow) with JSON Web Tokens (JWT).

1. **Obtain Credentials:** Request your client ID and secret from Novig.
2. **Request an Access Token:** Send a POST request to the OAuth endpoint. For example:
```bash
curl 
  --request POST 
  --url https://auth.novig.us/oauth/token 
  --header "Content-Type: application/json" 
  --data '{ 
    "audience"      : "https://api.novig.us", 
    "grant_type"    : "client_credentials", 
    "client_id"     : "YOUR_CLIENT_ID", 
    "client_secret" : "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET" 
  }'
```
3. **Use the Token:** Include the token in your requests by setting the HTTP header:
```bash
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN
```

> **Note:** For integration in the QA environment, you'll need to use different endpoints:
> - **Issuer:** `https://auth-qa.novig.us`
> - **Audience:** `https://api-qa.novig.us`
> - **API Base URL:** `https://api-qa.novig.us`

outcomeId.description: The server-generated UUID of the outcome for which the order will increase exposure

price.description: The price of the order in decimal probability, up to 3 decimal places

qty.description: The number of minimal currency units for the order. Note that this must be a positive integer. For CASH orders, 1 unit = 0.01 Novig Cash (e.g., 100 units = 1.00 Cash). For COIN orders, 1 unit = 1 Novig Coin

currency.description: Denomination of the order, i.e. `CASH` or `COIN`

ttl.description: Time to live for the order (milliseconds) after which the order will be automatically canceled if not totally filled. Applicable only under `GTT` time-in-force

flags.description: Custom 8-character string field for storing trader-specified flags or metadata about the order

id.description: The unique identifier of the order

outcomeId.description: The ID of the outcome associated with this order

marketId.description: The ID of the market associated with this order

price.description: The limit price at which the order is placed

qty.description: The remaining quantity of the order, denominated in Minimum Currency Units

originalQty.description: The original quantity of the order, denominated in Minimum Currency Units

currency.description: The currency in which the order is denominated

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the order was placed

status.description: The current status of the order

flags.description: The trader-specified metadata associated with the order

offset.description: Offset of the orders to return
Offset of the fills to return

orderId.description: The ID of the order that was filled

price.description: The price at which the fill was executed

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the fill occurred

isWash.description: Whether the fill is a wash trade

isTaker.description: Whether the fill is the taker

marketId.description: The ID of the related market

outcomeId.description: The ID of the related outcome

outcomeIds.description: Array of outcome IDs to filter positions by. If omitted, you will get all positions across open markets.

marketId.description: The ID of the market this position is for

outcomeId.description: The ID of the outcome this position is for

size.description: The quantity of positive exposure to the outcome

cost.description: The net entry cost of the position

status.description: The current status of the market (OPEN, CLOSED, or SETTLED)

strike.description: The threshold of the market in the unit of the stat type

type.description: The type of market (MONEY, SPREAD, TOTAL, RUSHING_ATTEMPTS, etc.)

volume.description: The total volume of the market

eventId.description: The event associated with the market

playerId.description: The player associated with the market (if applicable)

outcomeIds.description: Array of outcome IDs linked to this market

settledAt.description: When the market was settled (if applicable)

event.description: The event details associated with the market

price.description: The price of the order (in decimal probability, up to 3 decimal places)

qty.description: The remaining quantity of the order, denominated in Minimum Currency Units

originalQty.description: The original quantity of the order, denominated in Minimum Currency Units

outcomeId.description: The ID of the outcome for which the order is placed

bids.description: List of bid orders in price-time priority order

outcomeLadders.description: List of outcomes with their order books

offset.description: Offset of the transactions to return

balance.description: The balance amount available in the wallet.

openPositionCost.description: The cost locked in open positions.

openOrderCost.description: The cost locked in open orders.

credit.description: The credit amount available to the trader.

currency.description: The currency denomination of the associated wallet.

id.description: The unique identifier of the transaction

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the transaction was created

orderId.description: The associated order ID, if applicable

parlayId.description: The associated parlay ID, if applicable

marketId.description: The associated market ID, if applicable

data.description: The payload of the WebSocket message
The channel or market ID to subscribe to. Can be either a global channel name ('tape', 'lifecycle') or a UUID of an open market to receive updates only for that specific market.

channel.description: The channel or market ID that was subscribed to or unsubscribed from. Can be either a global channel name ('tape', 'lifecycle') or a UUID of a market.

game.description: The game associated with the event

description.example: KC Chiefs vs BUF Bills - Week 14

shortName.description: The short name of the team

scheduledStart.description: The scheduled start time of the game

homeTeam.description: The home team of the game

awayTeam.description: The away team of the game

league.description: The league to filter markets by

marketType.description: The type of market to filter by

orderId.description: The ID of the order that was filled

price.description: The effective price of the fill in decimal probability

isWash.description: Whether the fill is a wash trade

isTaker.description: Whether the fill is the taker

marketId.description: The ID of the related market
